text:
BOTTOM LINE — Modestly risk-on, small caps led, semis faded. Earnings optimism and a PayPal takeover rumor pushed tape higher, but SOXX -2.23% capped gains and highlighted a tech sub-sector divergence, leaving the day as a defensive rotation into value within a broadly flat session.

INDICES — VIX 15.7, down. IJR +0.54%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.40%, DIA +0.24%, SOXX -2.23%. Small > large by 0.14pp. Oil +1.01% led cross-asset. Neither SPY nor QQQ is below the 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.0x, QQQ 0.8x — QQQ is fading as price drops, low-conviction decline. SOXX at 1.1x with a -2.23% drop signals distribution in semis.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLC +1.73%, XLY +0.95%, XLF +0.68%, XLRE +0.18%, XLP +0.06%, XLV +0.00%, XLI -0.22%, XLB -0.28%, XLE -0.79%, XLU -1.03%, XLK -1.11%. Top 2 industries: KWEB +3.13%, MAGS +2.34%. Bottom 2: SOXX -2.23%, DRAM -6.26%. Money exited XLK and SOXX into XLC and XLF; rotation label: 'AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation'.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.31 — elevated protection demand. IV skew +3.8% shows mild put premium. VIX 15.7 below 20-day MA of 16.9 — complacency returning. No bond vol stress (MOVE unavailable).

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +1.01% vs XLE -0.79%, a notable divergence as energy stocks ignored crude’s gain. Dollar -0.49% helped small caps outperform.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 61, Greed. Top movers: PYPL +17.20% (vol 5.8x, takeover bid), BLK +6.63% (vol 2.1x, earnings), PGR -9.43% (vol 2.0x, earnings miss). Best 20d momentum: XLF, worst: SLV. PYPL vol spike = accumulation. Watch: ASML +2.23% (semis divergence), PGR -9.43% (distribution, break of trend), IBM -2.70% (vol spike, trend reversal).
